bindings.quest_action: expose the garrison's betrayal of our mutual defense pact
bindings.quest_focus: the broken oath between our settlement and the garrison
bindings.quest_stakes: our borders are undefended and stores are raided nightly
bindings.quest_threat: a sworn ally abandoned us without warning when we needed them most
bindings.quest_origin: a mutual defense pact signed three seasons ago
bindings.quest_reward_hint: if we prove the betrayal, the regional council will restore our protection

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, you don't know me, but I know when someone can be trusted. And right now I need someone I can trust more than anything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our settlement signed a mutual defense pact with the garrison at {location} three seasons ago. We supplied them with food, they protected our borders. Then the {enemy_type} raids started and the garrison pulled back to protect their own walls. {target_npc} rode to demand they honor the pact and was turned away at the gates. I need to {quest_action}, but first I need {quest_item} to fortify what's left and proof that {quest_focus} was deliberately violated.
dialogueChunks.outro: A sworn oath means nothing to them, but {quest_stakes}. Help me gather what we need to survive {quest_threat}.

playerResponses.text: Broken oaths deserve consequences. I'll help you build your case.
Gather supplies and evidence. I can do that.
Getting between a settlement and a garrison is too political for me.

bindings.quest_action: prove my business partner has been secretly selling our stock
bindings.quest_focus: the tavern we built together as equal partners
bindings.quest_stakes: our reputation is crumbling and regulars are leaving
bindings.quest_threat: a partner's greed is destroying everything we built on trust
bindings.quest_origin: a covenant sealed with a handshake and witnessed by the settlement

dialogueChunks.intro: I should be angry. I am angry. But right now, {player_name}, what I really am is hurt.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} and I built {quest_focus} together. Equal partners, equal shares. Last month, I discovered they've been secretly trading our best stock to {enemy_type} merchants {location_hint} for personal profit. I need to {quest_action} before {quest_stakes} gets any worse. I need {quest_item} to restock properly and evidence of the secret deals to bring before the settlement council.
dialogueChunks.outro: I trusted them with everything. {quest_threat}, and I want justice, not revenge. There's a difference.

playerResponses.text: Justice means evidence. I'll find it.
Betrayal by a partner cuts deep. I'll help you set things right.
Business disputes aren't really my area.

bindings.quest_action: retrieve the original trade covenant and expose the guild master's corruption
bindings.quest_focus: the merchant guild's five-year trade covenant
bindings.quest_stakes: local merchants are being undercut and ruined by side deals
bindings.quest_threat: the guild master has powerful friends and enforcers watching the roads
bindings.quest_origin: a trade agreement meant to ensure fair prices and shared routes
bindings.quest_time_pressure: the guild master is consolidating power and the evidence may vanish

dialogueChunks.intro: Come closer, {player_name}. This isn't a conversation for open air.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The merchant guild in {location} signed {quest_focus} five years ago: fair prices, shared routes, mutual support. Then the guild master started making side deals, undercutting everyone. {target_npc} uncovered the discrepancy. I need you to {quest_action}. Gather {quest_item} to support the merchants who've been cut out, and retrieve the original covenant from the guild hall {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Five years of trust, shattered by one person's greed. {quest_threat}, but {quest_focus} is still legally binding. If we can put it in front of the right people, this ends.

playerResponses.text: A binding covenant and a corrupt leader. I'll get you what you need.
Powerful friends and enforcers. This won't be easy, but I'm in.
Guild politics are a labyrinth I'd rather avoid.
